channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: This foren car hass veen there for last 2 days or more, 14th July
item.description:
This foren car there for quite long time bouth mirror broken and trayer flat inside look quite broken everything. parked in rong place in front of my shop chilli chill

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Riverdale Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London

Brambles overhanging on to path- happens every year

item.title: Leak from crack in highway, 14th July
item.description:
Despite hot weather this hasn't dried out clealry a leak of some description in services Been like this since end of last week

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Cedar Avenue, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London

item.title: Tree too top heavy., 14th July
item.description:
I’ve lived here for over 50 years. The tree has never been this big and top heavy. It has not been thinned for 5 years or more. I asked for it to be thinned a couple of years ago.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Coniston Road, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Jeep in car park, 14th July
item.description:
Jeep abandoned in car park of Copelands, 35 Copers Cope Road. Previously reported but ignored - investigation states that the vehicle was not present but it is not capable of being moved. Initial report came in January.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sellindge Close, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged man cover, 14th July
item.description:
The manhole cover has sunk and has been in this condition for a considerable period of time, with no update from the Council regarding what action will be taken to repair it. Vehicles are unable to pass over it properly, as the significant dip in the road has the potential to cause damage to cars. While the temporary repair has helped to some extent, as can be seen from the photographs, it is not a sustainable long-term solution. I would be grateful if clarification could be provided on when a permanent repair will be undertaken, as the current condition continues to present a risk of damage to vehicles using the road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Slade Green Road, Slade Green, Erith, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree blocking pathway and growing into thr road., 14th July
item.description:
This had occurred by the BT building and by The Town Hall Park entrance adjacent to Bole Court. The base of the tree has grown sign blocking the pathway and roadway

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cecil Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road grit, 14th July
item.description:
There is a shoal of road grit/ejecta from potholes on the carriageway, by the traffic island, posing a potentially deadly threat to cyclists and motorcyclists and a significant danger to cars.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Beckenham Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Several ebikes left at this location, 14th July
item.description:
At least 10 LIME and Forest bikes at junction of Lennard road and Kent house road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lennard Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish in unused bins, 14th July
item.description:
Various of these new bin containers have been placed around this area. But they weren’t set up with bins or a collection which has resulted in the bins being used technically fly tipping and nobody collecting. And in warm weather like this it will cause an issue. Please either have the bins removed or put in proper use

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 62, Canterbury Place, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping in Longmeadow road on grass area, 14th July
item.description:
Fly tipping just past number 48 on grass area. There is gas canister, blue lidded bexley bin and people have dumped their rubbish in it. This has been there for around 4 months.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 47, Longmeadow Road, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London

item.title: Dead tree, 14th July
item.description:
The tree opposite 37 Commerell Street Greenwich has died. Could it please be replaced. Thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Commerell Street, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Urgent – Fallen Tree Obstructing Maiden Lane and Immediate Vegetation Clearance Required, 14th July
item.description:
A tree has fallen onto Maiden Lane, further reducing the width of an already narrow road that is currently experiencing significant traffic congestion and a number of ongoing safety issues. This obstruction has made it even more difficult for vehicles, to pass safely through the area and requires urgent attention. In addition to removing the fallen tree, I would also request that the opportunity be taken to carry out a thorough inspection of the entire roadside verge and remove or cut back overgrown bushes and vegetation along the full length of this stretch of road. Overhanging branches are repeatedly coming into contact with passing vehicles, causing scratches and making an already restricted carriageway even narrower. For context, this location has been the subject of numerous concerns over recent years, including congestion, restricted visibility, oversized vehicles, and bridge-related safety issues. The fallen tree has further worsened an already challenging situation. I have attached photographs for reference, which I hope will assist in assessing the seriousness and urgency of the matter. I would appreciate it if this could be treated as a matter of priority and confirmation could be provided once the obstruction has been removed and the vegetation clearance has been completed. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Maiden Lane, Barnes Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on signs, 14th July
item.description:
Graffiti on signs

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lakeside Close, Blendon, Greater London

item.title: Overgrown hedges obstructing the pavement, 14th July
item.description:
323-337 Cavendish Road. The hedges are all overgrown and you cannot walk on the pavement with a pram or wheelchair anymore.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 319, Cavendish Road, Streatham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
